entry.title: Re: Prepare for the Future: Major SAP SuccessFactors Changes Coming in 2025 and 2026
entry.content: @lars_hentzen my apologies, corrected - Yes, as of today, SAP pushed the dates to later, still, customers are advised to migrate to newer reporting tools as SAP plans to phase out legacy reporting tools. While exact dates have not been published. But, as you know, Stories in People Analytics will replace all other SuccessFactors 'Legacy' reporting tools using a phased approach.

entry.title: Re: Is there a Method to translate CQN to resulting DB dependent SQL?
entry.content: Any Updates?

entry.title: Re: How to optimize the apps deployed on cloud foundry?
entry.content:
Hi @piyushvns21,

To assist you better, could you clarify whether your application is a CAP application with a Fiori front end or a custom SAP UI5 app?

Here are some useful resources:

  • For CAP: Performance Modeling in CAP
  • For SAP UI5:
    • UI5 Performance Optimization Guide
    • UI5 App Performance Guidelines
    • YouTube: Devtoberfest 2022 - Improving the Performance of UI5 Applications

If you can share more details about your application (e.g., specific libraries or components being used), it will be easier to provide more tailored advice.

Kind regards
Johannes

entry.title: Re: Webservice - grac_risk_analysis_wout_no_ws - how to get the composite role in the result?
entry.content: Any further ideas? Is it somehow possible to have the causing composite role in the result?

entry.title: Re: Request for Installation Media for NW AS ABAP 7.52 SP13

entry.title: New configuration Settlement Management in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi community,

I'm testing "Settlement Management" on S4 to replace "rebate" on ECC.
I'm doing some configuration, which I saw some support at https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/settlement-management-condition-contracts/ba-p/13484326.

I created a contract at tcode WCOCO similar to the link above.
Then I went to tcode WB2R_SC with this contract. At this moment I no longer have errors, but it does not generate any Credit Memo Request.


It looks like it only creates a temporary document.
I am using standard documents and numbering. What configuration will be missing?

 

Best Regards,

Daniel

entry.title: Re: Automation Pilot : Start & Stop HANA Instance, Schedule Executions on Automation Pilot
entry.content:
Yes start and stopping is available using the other environment.  The tutorial group https://developers.sap.com/group.sap-hana-cloud-automating.html provides examples using the CLI and SAP Automation Pilot for instances deployed to Cloud Foundry and to the subaccount (other environment).

Hope that helps,
Dan van Leeuwen

entry.title: Inspection type 89 'Other inspection' not available in Starter S/4HANA Cloud System
entry.content:
In system inspection type 89 is not available. Kindly help us with note or any solution which can help to get inspection type 89 into Starter system of SAP Public cloud.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Inspection type 89 'Other inspection' not available i...
entry.content:
The Inspection type 89 'Other inspection' is available with the activation of the scope item 'Nonconformance Management (2QN)'

 

Please activate the scope item by following the procedure outlined in the KBA https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3122430

entry.title: Re: Disenchantment: GPT for generating ABAP code documentation
entry.content: @luis_rod To be honest, I was more looking for other AI tools dedicated to this kind of work. I guess that Gemini and other GenAI engines won't do the job either, even if it looks better, it's far from what I'm expecting. Understanding code is not what AI can do for now, for now AI is just "rewording based on best statistical match".

entry.title: VPN Network Sizing for 300 user or 60 Fue
entry.content:
Hi ,

We are using S/4  HANA2023 in SAP Rise and we are having a 10Mbps VPN bandwidth  from Rise to Customer HO? Is the 10Mbps bandwidth  sufficient for handling 300 user or 60 Fue ? On what basis we can size the Network bandwidth for S/4  HANA users in SAP rise? Help highly appreciated.

entry.title: Account 'KTNAFB : Acc.Dep. Accnt.for Ordinary Dep' could not be found for area 30 Message no. AU133
entry.content:
While doing Asset settlement AUC to main asset not able to settled same asset class i have done one AUC but other not able to do 

i have check all configuration is correct but still facing same issue  

Account 'KTNAFB : Acc.Dep. Accnt.for Ordinary Dep' could not be found for area 30

Message no. AU133

Diagnosis

The system could not find account 'KTNAFB : Acc.Dep. Accnt.for Ordinary Dep' for company code XXXX in depreciation area 30 when creating the journal entry.

Procedure

1. Check whether a G/L account is defined in the account determination of Asset Accounting (in Customizing for Asset Accounting for example under Assign G/L Account or under Define Offsetting Account for Legacy Data Transfer 

  1. Check whether the defined G/L account is created in your company code.
  2. Define a G/L account or create the G/L account in your company code.

SAP FI Asset Accounting

Regards 

Ajay

entry.title: Re: Implement Currency Conversion in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
can you please confirm if the SAP Cloud Connector has to be enable for importing from an S4/HANA onprem system. it is not obvious in any SAP official documentation that i can find.

 

thanks

g

entry.title: How to display page header on start of every page on crystal report ?
entry.content:
Issue with Page Header Display in Crystal Report

I am working on a Crystal Report that contains 5 pages. I need to display the page header at the start of page when finish .

The page header displays correctly on the first page. However, on subsequent pages, the page header appears at the bottom of the page instead of the top.

 

report contain to 3 section 

page header

sub report1

subreport2

suppose my report display 2 companies 

first company take two page 

second company will start from third page but this not happen 

it  start from second page and this is my issue 

I need the page header to display at the top of Page when start.

meaning it will start from page third on top to be as second company

How can I achieve this?

entry.title: Re: Why do I still report an error after using a dynamic DELETE statement?
entry.content:
Hi, I think your mistake is enclosing the actual condition you use in the WHERE clause in single quotes. Try removing them and check the results.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Sci-Kit learn library in SAP Datasphere Data Flow
entry.content:
Hi @Moritz_F, the Python operator in a Datasphere Data Flow supports only the Python packages listed here. Further packages cannot be installed. Please also consider that the Python operator receives the data in packages. You are not guaranteed to get all data at once. Hence I see two options:

  1. Consider whether your scikit-learn logic can be implemented using the HANA Machine Learning, that is part of Datasphere's HANA Cloud. Here is an example https://community.sap.com/t5/artificial-intelligence-and-machine-learning-blogs/hands-on-tutorial-machine-learning-with-sap-datasphere/ba-p/13796417
  2. Alternatively you would need to extract the data to a Python environment (ie in BTP: Jupyter Notebooks in Business Application Studio or Python in Cloud Foundry) and write any output back to Datasphere

entry.title: Add an accounting document to an SD sales flow
entry.content:
Hello!

I would like some help, we need to make an accounting entry referring to a document billed by SD, but it will be a manual entry, I would like to know if there is the possibility of adding this entry to the SD flow, in VF...
Is there a more Standard way possible, and if it's just Z, how would I do it?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PT BR

Ola!

Gostaria de uma ajuda, precisamos fazer um lançamento contábil referente a um documento faturado por SD, mas será um lançamente manual, gostaria de saber se existe a possibilidade de adicionar esse lançamento no fluxo de SD, na VF...
Existe algum modo mais Standard possível, e se for somente Z, como eu faria?

entry.title: Wrong conversion of amount to condition value in Purchase Order (SAP S/4Hana)
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,

Can you help me with the following issue? For our customer, we are doing a brown field conversion from ECC to S/4Hana (2023 – FPS02).

After the conversion to S/4, I have noticed unexpected behaviour in the PO pricing. There are two issues:

  1. There is a difference by a factor of 1000 between the entered amount and the calculated condition value.
  2. The Net price automatically rounds to full numbers.

Below is an example of such a PO price. This is also the same for other condition types such as PB00.

In the example below, the desired result was an order of 100 pieces at 987.61 EUR per piece, so 98 761 EUR. However, SAP rounds up to 988 and divides the unit price by 1000.

This results in a net price of 99 EUR for the PO item. This is not correct

 

I have reviewed the condition type PBXX and PB00 and cannot find any strange settings. The condition settings were taken over as is from the original system.

 

 

I have also verified if it was not a requirement that was causing this, but I could not find any requirements responsible for converting to this.

I have also verified the currency settings and have not seen anything out of the ordinary.

Please assist,

Thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition & SAP Mobile St...
entry.content: Hello @adityaarora, thank you for the reply. The aim is to switch between the available Build Work Zone page languages, not the SAP Start Mobile languages. This is something available when you open the page on the desktop browser, but not when you open the same page in SAP Mobile Start.

entry.title: Dear Expert,When we try to create a Generic Material Code from MM41 we are facing issue "
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

When we try to create a Generic Material Code from MM41 we are facing issue "

Cannot use conf. class ZBED_CLASS, class type 300 for generic article creation

Message no. MH336"

We are using Variant class 300 and assigned with Multiple characters Like Color, Size, Other few Characters. Can we assign multiple characters in Variant class300,is this issue for throwing above error.

entry.title: What are the ways to connect SAP PO to SQL DB ?
entry.content:
Hi,

The requirement is to send data from SAP ECC to SQL DB. We have the option of connecting to SQL DB via JDBC adapter. But the client is insisting on using HTTPS connectivity. 

How can this be achieved? 

Regards,

LR

Santosh

entry.title: Re: error : failed to fetch data
entry.content: Use RSRT to re-generate the queries after BW change. This will solve the "failed to fetch data..." issue.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hi @EVass sounds great. I want to introduce you to SAP others in Texas @rileyrainey, SAP Mentor @DiegoDora, @brian_dennett @SimonTo  and SAP champion @SergioG_TX.

If you haven't yet you probably want to join the Dallas - SAP Community location group!

Also, want to connect you with my fellow community advocates @lauraschmitz @KatherineKenneally 

Best of luck and see you in the community!

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center: New way to view, download and transport configurations
entry.content:
Hi @hariprasadgud 

I'm working on transporting 22 roles today and ran into errors like:

  • IJ031070: Transaction cannot proceed: STATUS_MARKED_ROLLBACK
  • Wrapped Exception: DAOException caught: IJ031070: Transaction cannot proceed: STATUS_MARKED_ROLLBACK
  • SQLException caught: IJ031070: Transaction cannot proceed: STATUS_MARKED_ROLLBACK

I guess the cause for these errors is too many roles in 1 bundle. Can you confirm? And if so, what exactly is the limitation? I now only see it is not working on validating the import. Would be nice if I would get the error earlier so I can update the bundle. Assuming the size of changes is the cause.

I redid the transport with only 10 roles: no issue
2nd part with 12 roles: same issue for the last 3

Would be great to get some insights around this.

Thanks!
Roos🌹

I created a button and run a JS flow function with your code:

 

I added the code (return) so that the flow function would output the answer to the next flow functions, but it even worked without.

Thank you,
Davide

entry.title: How to create picking warehouse tasks for an outbound delivery order on RF device?
entry.content:
Hello!
I have one question on SAP S4/HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Is it possible to create picking warehouse tasks for an outbound delivery order on RF device? If so, how can I do it?

Many times I´ve created putaway warehouse tasks for an inbound delivery on RF device (Inbound Process -> Receiving of HUs -> Receiving HU by delivery). I´d like to do the same for picking warehouse tasks for an outbound delivery on RF, but can´t find a way.

Best Regards

entry.title: The most suitable book
entry.content: "Regarding an ERP system novice, what is the most recommended book?"

entry.title: Re: New "Wait for API Call" Integrates Processes with External Systems
entry.content:
Thanks for comments.

When you say agent, I am assuming you are talking about automation (iRPA). But this is a process step, not an automation activity. My understanding is that the process instance completely stops until the API call is made.

entry.title: Re: Accept API payment through External Cash Desk via SAP BTP
entry.content:
Nikhil, 

Try to explore the SAP DPAO Functionality once rather than External Cash Desk, if SAP DPAO is under the S4 Contract, it has much more enhanced features than External cash desk.

On the other hand, with external cash desk, you have to do the below config settings 

Further more you have to activate the ODATA Services for 

(a) Accept and Integrating Payments

(b) Payment Status Determination

(c) Payment reversal

(d) Asynchronous Bank responses.

Thanks

Amlan

entry.title: Re: Electronic Invoicing in Malaysia
entry.content:
Hi Members,

Would you please let me know where we can store the IRBM number in the FI document number. As IRBM is 26 characters & we don't have any field available at header level with 26 characters. We are not using the full (DRC) solution provided by SAP.

 

Thank in advance

entry.title: Re: issue with java script action in Build apps

entry.title: Re: Leave roll over for all employees to 31st march 2025
entry.content: Can you Share the Rule

entry.title: Re: Electronic Invoicing in Malaysia
entry.content:
Hello adityajan,

there is no option to not use the full solution and some kind of partial construct for DRC. DRC consists of the XML creation portion and the end-to-end submission to the recipient. It requires the license for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition and cannot be circumvented. Anything else is neither recommended nor supported by SAP. I am rather curious who informed you that a partial solution is available, perhaps you can share for the benefit of the other people on this thread as well.

Thanks,

Martin

entry.title: Re: issue with java script action in Build apps
entry.content: Might help to see more of that JS flow and the way you are getting it inside the next JS dlow.

entry.title: Nonconformance AI-based Insights in SAP Digital Manufacturing PODs
entry.content:
Disclaimer: This is a personal custom (in-progress) development for learning purposes.

To begin my learning journey with AI Agents, I came up with the following storyline:

An AI Agent that keeps an eye in the current shift nonconformances, provide AI-based insights and recommendations, and with some reasoning, decides and take some proactive and automatic actions, like raising alerts to collaborate with supervisors and maintenance team, or trigger actions in other solutions or external systems, if any support seems to be needed.

Today, I reached the very first milestone of my story: "Keep an eye in the current shift nonconformances and provide AI-based insights and recommendations".

To achieve that, I used SAP Digital Manufacturing, SAP AI Core and SAP Generative AI Hub, SAP BTP and python to build the following orchestration logic:

  1. Retrieve Nonconformances from SAP Digital Manufacturing via /v1/nonconformances API for the current shift.
  2. Prompt a trained model in SAP AI Core / SAP Generative AI Hub via API, providing the nonconformances as input.

Which is deployed in SAP BTP, and called from SAP Digital Manufacturing PODs via an Action Button, to display the AI-based insights and recommendations on the fly using POD Messages, as shown below:

In my experience so far, hallucinations are rare and the insights and recommendations make sense most of the time. However, moving forward, I will explore some grounding possibilities, to reduce the risk of hallucinations and improve the overall responses.

More to come, hopefully soon! Stay tuned. 🙂

Learn more about Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Core, see diagram below:

Br, Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: How to fetch the documents attached to Inspection met...
entry.content:
Greetings,

can you try table DRAD. There you can find document assigned to object. You can filter by object type (QMTBDOC) and object key. Object key is [PLANT][METHOD][VERSION].

Best regards, M

entry.title: An error msg. id F2003 as "This account XXXX is one-time" in Tcode XK02,
entry.content:
Hi Guys,

While entering customer in control view of vendor master record in Tcode XK02,

System prompts an error msg. id F2003 as "This account XXXX is one-time".

We are going to migrate to S/4 HANA from ECC.

Please guide me to solve this.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: New configuration Settlement Management in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi @daniel_cunha1, 

In the preceding screen, please ensure that you select "Live Run" as the Run Type (under the Program Run Control section).

Additionally, in SAP Settlement Management, the system does not create a Credit Memo Request in SD as it would in ECC. Instead, it generates a Settlement Document with a direct posting to FI. There is also an option in the configuration to prevent the automatic release of the FI document if needed, providing greater control over the process.

Welcome to the SAP Settlement Management module! It features a completely different architecture and agreement management approach compared to SAP ECC SD rebates, offering enhanced flexibility and capabilities.

Best regards,
Gauthier

entry.title: SAP Fieldglass: Learn More About New and Consolidated Features
entry.content:
Hello! In this blog, we explore both new and established SAP Fieldglass features. Keeping up with changes can be challenging, but we're here to help. We've created two videos to guide you through new features that enhance your SAP Fieldglass experience. For those interested in familiar features, we've prepared seven guidebooks in PDF format. Whether you're curious about new updates or want to review existing features, these resources will help you make the most of SAP Fieldglass.

 

Data Privacy

Video Tutorial

In this video, we show how to access the main features related to data privacy in SAP Fieldglass. 

Link: https://video.sap.com/media/t/1_qo0x7cii 

0:09 - Configurations needed to change data privacy settings 

0:48 - How to access the Personally Identifiable Information (PII) in your system 

1:09 - How to navigate the PII report 

1:56 - How to manually erase data 

2:46 - How to set the PII thresholds lookup  

3:45 - How to use the PII thresholds lookup output 

 

What's New (2405 Release)

Video Tutorial

This video highlights two great features introduced in release 2405: SAP Companion and Enhanced Sign-in Assistant. 

Link: https://video.sap.com/media/t/1_ctaslpvk

0:06 - SAP Companion 

0:51 - Enhanced Sign-in Assistant 

 

Consolidated Features 

Guidebooks

Here, you can download guidebooks explaining how to configure and use a few well-established features in SAP Fieldglass, such as data sets and SOW Bids:

  • SAP Fieldglass - Data Sets 
  • SAP Fieldglass - Tax Management Features 
  • SAP Fieldglass - SOW Bids 
  • SAP Fieldglass - Tenure Policy Management 
  • SAP Fieldglass - Statement of Work (SOW) Characteristics 
  • SAP Fieldglass - Company Structure (Guide for Buyers) 
  • SAP Fieldglass - Rates 2.0   

 

Questions? Post them as comments!

Hari

entry.title: Re: How to get certificate to our personal mail id
entry.content:
Hi @Anusha1224 
It looks like this is a new question. 
I would like to encourage to always use our Ask Questions section and post your question there.
This makes it easier  to follow-up:
That way other learners can benefit from the answer:
If you ask the question there other learners  can search for it in the future if they run into the same issue and there we have more features available for responses.
Would you mind to repost it ?

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: An Introduction to Documentation Versions on the SAP Help Portal for Functional Localization
entry.content:
I’m pleased to share the latest information on accessing documentation on the SAP Help Portal for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. In this blog post, I’ll explain how you can choose the right documentation version for your technical requirements and discuss the recent updates on the SAP Help Portal for maintenance phases.

Title image for the topic of documentation versions on the SAP Help Portal

I’ll be focusing today on the country/region-specific documentation for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition that is published through the Documentation Delivery with SAP Notes process, though there are useful tips here for everyone who browses version-specific content on the SAP Help Portal.

1. Choosing the Right Documentation Version for You

The SAP Help Portal is your number-one resource for information on SAP products. When navigating documentation, it’s important to verify that the version you’re browsing is correct for your solution. You can do this by checking the Version Selector drop-down menu:

Drop-down version selection on the SAP Help Portal

Why do we recommend this version verification step? Well, you can access SAP Help Portal documentation by many different means, such as links from SAP Notes and What’s New topics, as well as from search engines. You may be directed to the highest version, which is not necessarily the same version implemented by your company. Different versions may have different scope functionality described in the documentation, so it’s best to always check this drop-down menu when accessing the SAP Help Portal.

2. New on the SAP Help Portal: Maintenance Information

You might have noticed that, when you access documentation for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, there’s a new information banner that specifies the maintenance phase:

Maintenance message now available on the SAP Help Portal

This information banner differentiates between two maintenance phases, Mainstream Maintenance and Extended Maintenance. If you are an SAP customer or partner with a valid SAP S-User ID, you can refer to the SAP Maintenance Phases resource for further details on this topic.

For the most recent documentation versions on the SAP Help Portal, you are informed that “documentation is updated with the support package stacks”. However, there is an important exception to this rule for country/region-specific updates delivered for functional localization.

3. Always Up-to-Date: Documentation Delivery for Functional Localization

For country/region-specific development delivered for functional localization, such as legal changes, a new process provides documentation updates between support package stacks. As detailed in the recent blog post on Documentation Delivery on the SAP Help Portal with SAP Notes, you now have access to SAP Help Portal documentation that reflects the latest information, regardless of the planned support package release dates.  

For both the mainstream and extended maintenance phases of SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, we’re glad to provide you with up-to-date information on how SAP meets the local legal requirement for your solution. 

To find more information on country/region-specific topics, I recommend the Localization topic page on the SAP Community. There you can find the latest news, resources, and upcoming events for SAP’s localized solutions. Remember, too, that you can always select the Create a Question option on the Localization topic page to engage with experts and resolve any doubts you may have:

Create a Question for Localization on the SAP Community

And for those of you who use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, you can find more details on the SAP Help Portal and SAP Community in our dedicated blog series:

  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Engaging with the SAP Community

That’s it for today, though please let me know in the comments section if you have any questions. Your feedback is always appreciated.

Thank you and until next time,

Barnabas

entry.title: Re: issue with java script action in Build apps
entry.content:
hi @Dan_Wroblewski ,

here are the SS, for the flow and the input

entry.title: Re: Invoicing in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Hello @MOHAMMED_TAMBOLI 

Thanks for your kind words. Request you to refer point '6. cXML Upload' in the 'Invoice Entry by Supplier' section of this post itself. Additionally, please refer SAP Ariba APIs for more details on available Ariba APIs.

entry.title: Xblnr_alt update via function module or IRBM no field for malaysia
entry.content:
Hello Members,

Would you please let us know the functional module to update the XBLNR_ALT field to update the IRBM number for Malaysia.

We are not using  the solution provided by SAP for Malaysia e-invoicing.
We are trying to use XBLNR_ALT field to update the IRBM number via FM. If there is any other field we can use to store the IRBM number in the FI document number, please let us know.

thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: can't open this link - replicate SQLA to msSQL
entry.content:
The Doc Comment Exchange (DCX) system was retired at the end of 2023. That content is available on SAP Help SQL Anywhere. As an aside, this question has references to SQL Anywhere 16 content. That version has reached End of Mainstream Maintenance (EOMM). 

See my response in your original question which provides links to relevant content for SQL Anywhere 17.  Here is the link to Synchronization Models as my previous response points to the entire document where this content is provided.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition & SAP Mobile St...
entry.content: @PetraMi On the desktop browser, please select the "Browser Language" option under the Language and Region settings. Once this is configured, follow the steps I shared earlier on your mobile device. By selecting one of the supported languages (e.g., Croatian or English in your case), the content should be displayed in that language within SAP Mobile Start. Please note that there might be a slight delay before the updated language is reflected in the app.

entry.title: Wanted to Hide Form Settings in the Menu Bar in SAP Business One
entry.content: I wanted to hide the Form Settings button in the Main Menu marked in the attached screenshot, is there a way in SAP Business One for 10.0 version for SAP HANA (10.00.240) SP 2402 (Security) HOTFIX1 (64-bit) ?

entry.title: Re: Disenchantment: GPT for generating ABAP code documentation
entry.content:
Sandra:

Absolutely, AI still has a long way to go in fully understanding code, and maybe that's a good thing—it gives us more time before we're replaced! 😊

IBM is taking an interesting approach with their AI tools for the IBM i series. Many of these systems still use old RPG programs, and the programmers who wrote them are now retired or about to retire. To address this, IBM is developing AI tools specifically designed to explain old code, even to non-RPG programmers. They have asked their community to provide source code examples to help train their model, and they plan (I believe) to release a beta tool this year.

Who knows? Maybe SAP will offer a similar tool in the not-so-distant future.

Best regards,

Luis

entry.title: Re: Striking a Balance: Navigating the Open-Source Tightrope in a World of Evolving Threats - Part 1
entry.content:
Hi @Ni4_mhjn,

thanks for your comment and also reminding me that I forgot to mention that we plan a blog about how we're addressing such risks in a later blog. I added it to the blog right now. 
Re: your question about 'trustworthiness'. There are multiple ways to address that, one example is the OpenSSF Scorecard. Here's an exempt from their webpage: "The OpenSSF Scorecard assesses open source projects for security risks through a series of automated checks. It was created by OSS developers to help improve the health of critical projects that the community depends on."


Cheers
Philip

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
hi @EVass welcome to the #SAPCommunity - hope you can join the Dallas group as @StephanieMarley mentioned. we have 2-3 events per year to help the local people connect, learn and share their knowledge. Hope to see you in one of those this year,

Sergio

entry.title: IBP Excel Add in "Auto fit Column" check box
entry.content:
Dear all, 
 in IBP Excel adding under settings >> Options >>display tab, there is a  checkbox for auto fit . 
 
one of the client complained , post 2411 upgrade  this checkbox got selected by default   but users dont need it . 
 
one option is : 
go to each template, each workbook and remove the check box, save and update the template. 
 
and same need to be done for all the Favorites  created by any user.
 
Question : is there any global parameter which can by-pass this setting so that we need not to go to all the templates /worksheets manually and uncheck the box ?

entry.title: All That You Can't Leave Behind
entry.content:
In the year 2000, Irish rock band U2 released their 10th studio album, All That You Can't Leave Behind. The album was seen as a return to the band's "traditional sound" after spending the 1990's following their classic The Joshua Tree (1987) with more experimental sounds on Achtung Baby (1991), Zooropa (1993), and Pop (1997). In a similar way, the forthcoming release of SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 can also be seen as a return to tradition as the suite focuses on its most popular applications, Crystal Reports and Web Intelligence. Some SAP BusinessObjects customers no doubt have felt stuck in a moment they couldn't get out of, spending years wondering if SAP was ever going to get the BusinessObjects band back together, unimpressed with the songs about SAP Analytics Cloud (all except Cloudspotter by the Foo Fighters), and tempted by the siren songs of Tableau and Microsoft Power BI.

But many SAP BusinessObjects customers recognize that March 12, 2025 is going to be a beautiful day. SAP is giving the world something that simultaneously seems familiar yet thoroughly modernized, both in terms of its technological underpinnings as much as its modern analytics user experience (UX). And just like some of U2's best work, we will still be enjoying these tools into the next decade.

The Past

Having been through the experience of retiring Desktop Intelligence and Adobe Flash-based apps like Dashboards (Xcelsius), there are some applications and technologies currently in SAP BI 4.3 that aren't going to be present in BI 2025 and this article will review the business impact of SAP's decisions and what customers need to do to be ready for the updated platform.

Adobe Flash-based Content

The Adobe Flash player was retired in December 2020, but we'll start with this old news for sake of completeness. SAP stopped supporting Adobe Flash-based content with the release of SAP BI 4.2 SP9, but customers may have not undertaken the effort to remove the content, as it simply stopped working when Adobe retired its Flash player. SAP KB 2914654 provides a Java program that customers can download and run on their SAP BusinessObjects systems. The script will identify and remove all Desktop Intelligence, Dashboards/Xcelsius, and Explorer from their systems. Oddly enough, the script does not delete Adobe Flash objects, possibly because SAP felt it best to only remove its own content types. The script also won't delete any empty folders left behind. So, there's possibly a larger opportunity to manually remove Adobe Flash files and obsolete directories from the Central Management Console. The script has a commit operation at the end, so you can run it one time without committing and examine the log to see exactly what will be deleted when you do commit.

I recommend giving this script a run on your current system. I was able to run it on a fairly large SAP BusinessObjects installation that had quite a bit of leftover Dashboards/Xcelsius content and even one orphaned Desktop Intelligence object in the CMS (although the Deski file itself had been deleted from the file repository long ago). The Dashboards content had its own folder structure, which required separate clean-up of the folders and the SWF files left untouched by the script.

Crystal Reports and Crystal Reports for Enterprise

No, Crystal Reports is not being retired. Not by a long shot. But there will be some changes in BI 2025 that customers should be prepared for prior to upgrading to BI 2025.

First, SAP BusinessObjects customers on SAP BI 4.3 SP2 and higher already know that Crystal Reports is no longer supported on Linux servers - only Microsoft Windows Server (see SAP KB 3109523 and 3362867). I helped a customer with around 200 "active" Crystal Reports upgrade to BI 4.3 SP2 on Linux. As expected, the schedules for the Crystal Reports stopped running. What was not expected? Nobody ever called to see what happened to their Crystal Reports. Linux customers can continue support simply by adding some Windows servers to their existing Linux cluster. 

Second, SAP Crystal Reports for Enterprise is deprecated and will not be part of the BI 2025 platform. A short history lesson - Crystal Reports for Enterprise was introduced with SAP BI 4.0 in 2010. It was intended to be a "modern" version of Crystal Reports, built on Java and Eclipse instead of Windows and C/C++. It began by introducing UNX support and slowly gained new features to help retire traditional Crystal Reports. So now in a small course correction, UNX support was added to Crystal Reports 2020 SP4 and higher (SAP KB 1644732).

SAP BusinessObjects Mobile

SAP BusinessObjects Mobile will end support on December 31, 2025 and will not be supported in BI 2025. Although other analytics apps such as SAP Analytics Cloud, Microsoft Power BI, Tableau, ThoughtSpot and others are maintaining their mobile apps, SAP has transitioning its BusinessObjects platform to a web responsive platform that supports mobile browsers without the need of an app. See some alternative strategies in Gregory Bottichio's May 2024 SAP Community article, SAP BusinessObjects Mobile alternatives.

UNV is Dead. Long live UNX.

The Information Design Tool and its UNX semantic layer was introduced with SAP BI 4.0 in 2010 and intended to eventually replace the UNV semantic layer. Similar to Crystal Reports for Enterprise that shipped alongside it, the Information Design Tool was built on a modern Java/Eclipse framework. In the 15 years since the introduction of the Information Design Tool, many customers have already embraced the UNX format. After many years of support, the UNV format will not be supported in BI 2025, so if you have any active universes remaining in this format, conversion is required. There are lots of resources in the SAP Support Portal (SAP KB 3291488 and 3350459, among many others). Customers with large numbers of UNV universes or reports that point to them will want to investigate multiple third-party tools that will do the heavy lifting.

Well, Except for UNX Multi-Source Universes

SAP BI 4.0 was introduced with its Data Federator technology embedded to support multi-source universes. While data federation as a marketing concept seemed like a great idea (don't bother moving data to a central data warehouse, just stitch it together with federation) it never really took off, neither from SAP or other data federation vendors. Unfortunately, a multi-source universe is like the dark side of the Force. Remember when your coworkers warned you that "if once you start down the dark path of multisource universes, forever will it dominate your destiny. Consume you it will!" Sadly, there's no automated way (unless SAP is going to provide one with BI 2025?) to convert a multi-source universe back to single source - you simply must rebuild it as single source.

Live Office

Live Office, a plug-in to Microsoft Office, seemed like a good idea at the time. It did its job well, but many SAP BusinessObjects customers shunned it simply on the basis that it required software to be installed on user desktop. Browser-based tools were the future and deploying Web Intelligence Rich Client, Live Office, etc. just wasn't going to be allowed by corporate IT.

Oh look - what's this shiny new desktop app? Well, it's Tableau Desktop. Quick! Let's install it before corporate IT finds out.

Live Office will not be in BI 2025 and Laura Vega has written a helpful SAP Community article, SAP BusinessObjects Live Office Alternatives

Lumira

SAP Lumira, first introduced as SAP Visual Intelligence, was introduced at SAPPHIRE in 2012 to great fanfare, followed shortly by SAP Design Studio. These apps were later brought together as a family in July 2017, with SAP Lumira Desktop becoming SAP Lumira Discovery 2.0 and SAP Design Studio becoming SAP Lumira Designer 2.0. Neither of these tools has received much enhancement since the initial 2.0 fanfare. Accordingly, SAP Lumira Discovery 2.x will not be supported in BI 2025. In contrast, SAP Lumira Designer 2.0 will be supported to appease some very loud or noisy (or both?) customers. However, supported does not mean enhanced.

The Future

While the deprecation of legacy tools always brings some level of disappointment, SAP is responding to where most its customers were headed anyway. This article has focused largely on the past. In my next SAP Community article, we'll talk about the future - combining SAP's powerful universe semantic layer with thoroughly modern cloud-based databases like Databricks, Snowflake and Trino. Those platforms are already supported in BI 4.3 - let's hope the BI 2025 platform support matrix, once released, has some interesting new things in it.

For the customers that rely on Crystal Reports and Web Intelligence, these tools are thoroughly modernized with an emphasis on user experience and usability. March 12, 2025 is truly going to be a beautiful day. Don't let it get away.

While other analytics vendors are using 2025 to raise their prices, SAP and its cadre of passionate partners can help you continue to use the BusinessObjects platform and deliver solutions with a lower total cost of ownership. What you don't have, you don't need it now.

What legacy technology still lurks in YOUR BusinessObjects platform that's going to need retirement before BI 2025? And how do you feel about the future of BusinessObjects? Will March 12, 2025 be a beautiful day for your organization? Or do you feel stuck in a moment you can't get out of? Let's continue the conversation in the comments below.

References

SAP KB 1644732 - Unable to create report on a UNX Universe in Crystal Reports

SAP KB 2914654 - Script to delete deprecated content from BI platform repository after update to BI 4.3 or BI 4.2 SP09 and above

SAP KB 3109523 - Deprecation of Crystal Reports 2020 services on Linux and Unix

SAP KB 3291488 - BI 4.x: Information on Migrating from unv to unx Universes

SAP KB 3350459 - Support of Universe Design Tool : UNV is dead, long live UNX

SAP KB 3362867 - Crystal Reports 2020 services on Windows deleted after upgrade on Linux/Unix in distributed environment

SAP Community - SAP BusinessObjects Live Office Alternatives

SAP Community - SAP BusinessObjects Mobile alternatives

entry.title: Re: Add an accounting document to an SD sales flow
entry.content:
In SAP, creating a manual accounting entry that integrates with the SD (Sales and Distribution) billing flow is possible, but it depends on the specific requirements and the level of integration needed. Here’s a breakdown:

Standard Approach Options:

1. User Exit / BAdI for VF01/VF02 (Standard Billing): SAP provides several user exits or Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) that allow you to enhance the billing process without modifying the standard system. For example:

User Exit: USEREXIT_ACCOUNT_PREP_KOMKCV or USEREXIT_ACCOUNT_PREP_KOMPCV in the billing document. These are located in the enhancement RV60AFZZ.

BAdI: AC_DOCUMENT or SD_VPRS_ACCOUNTING.

How it works:

You can add logic in these exits to create additional accounting entries while the billing document is being processed.

This keeps the process integrated with the billing flow and ensures consistency.

2. Accounting Adjustment in FI Post-Billing:

If you need to make a manual adjustment after the billing document is posted, you can use transaction FB01 to post directly to FI.

However, this entry would not be tied directly to the SD billing document unless additional logic (e.g., referencing billing doc in text fields) is added.

---

Customized (Z) Approach:

If standard options do not fully meet your needs, you can create a custom solution:

1. Enhancements in Billing Document Flow:

Use a custom ABAP program to insert logic that triggers additional accounting postings when a billing document is created (via VF01 or VF02).

Link this logic to the billing document (e.g., using the billing document number in the custom table or referencing the SD flow).

2. Steps to Create Custom Z Logic:

Identify the point of enhancement: Use BAdIs or user exits mentioned above.

Create a custom program: Develop a Z-program to perform the manual posting (similar to FB01) but triggered programmatically during billing document processing.

Integration with SD Flow: Store the link between the billing document and the manual accounting entry in custom tables for reporting and audit purposes.

---

Recommendation:

If possible, use the Standard User Exit or BAdI approach to maintain integration and avoid unnecessary customization. Custom (Z) solutions should only be used if the standard options cannot fulfill your business requirements.

entry.title: Re: How do i calculate the data between the manage systems and Solution Manager
entry.content: It may help other address the question if you can share the use case (e.g., system monitoring) and the underlying concern (e.g., storage requirements or sizing).

entry.title: Re: SAP BPC End of Life (?)
entry.content: Hello Deb_D, I don't know about it. But there might be differences depending on the Product version you are using. You could have a look at the PAM, link is also in the mentioned article..

entry.title: Reset the value of MV_FIR_STATUS of FM: ME_READ_HEADER_INVOICE_FIR
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

is there a Standard FM or Standard Program that can reset the value of MV_FIR_STATUS of FM: ME_READ_HEADER_INVOICE_FIR ?

entry.title: Re: Import Letter of Credit - TRM - Treasury - Complete Process including Config and Front end Proce
entry.content: HI, is the functionality of SAP TRM and SAP Trade Finance included in the S4/HANA current Core or is an additional licence needed if Letter of Credit needs to be implemented? Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP BPC End of Life (?)
entry.content: thank you

entry.title: Re: Increase in Non Completions for online Learning items...
entry.content:
I would look at users' browser settings.

The browser must allow third party cookies. In Safari, the setting "Prevent Cross-site Tracking" should be OFF.

Without the correct settings, users will time out after 30 minutes, but the LMS will not indicate such when a user in in a lesson. It will allow them to finish, but it will not send any further progress or completion data. Users will find that they either have to start over, or start around the 30 min mark where they timed out.

This has been our #1 support issue for years. Its getting worse as browsers dont allow 3rd party cookies by default. Most of our users use their own devices, or are on iOS devices, neither of which we can control the browser settings. 

A place to send users to check settings is https://www.whatismybrowser.com/ It has a check to see if 3rd party cookies are enabled (and lots of ads).

entry.title: Re: Polluted by many questions NOT about SAP Websites in...
entry.content: @Sandra_Rossi - this is already on our list of enhancements, and also something we are already working on. I will get back to you know as soon as we have more to share.

entry.title: Re: Leave roll over for all employees to 31st march 2025
entry.content: @sikindar_a sure If This rule is always true. To add an expression please uncheck the Always True checkbox. Then Set Time Account.Booking Possible Until to be equal to 03/31/9999

entry.title: Re: Bulk Upload Items in LMS
entry.content:
Hello @Hamza599 ,

Assuming that you would want to mass upload content files to iContent and create entities, here is the KBA:2568384 - How to mass upload content files to iContent and create entities? - SAP for Me

But I would like to inform you that this will not work for SCORM 2004 contents as it needs to be uploaded from LMS itself.

Another option for Content Import:

  • SAP offers iContent Premium Service that consists to have a team specialized in importing content and creating items for our customers. For further information, the customer's SAP Sales Representative and/or Customer Success Manager can be reached.

If this information helped you then please consider giving Kudos or accepting this post as solution.

Swapnil

entry.title: Re: Partner Consolidation Unit is blank in ACDOCA
entry.content: Trading Partner is Company rather than Company Code. Please check the Partner Consolidation Unit derivation, including definition of consolidation unit as well as the GRPL substituion.

entry.title: Advice on Curriculum Basis Date for clients new global SF-LMS instance
entry.content:
Hi there,

I have a question in regards to the Curriculum Basis Date in Global Application Settings. The client i am working for already have SF-LMS Module. In their existing BAU LMS module, which they"ve had for a number of years, they have configured the Curriculum Basis Date to 01/Jan/2010. 

Since the Client is a global company with offices in many geographies, they are in the process of rolling out a brand new SF system (incl LMS) to their global workforce. As part of configuring the system and setting it up, I have a question in relationt to whether the Curriculum Basis Date (Global Application Settings) in the to-be SF-LMS instance should retain the original curriculum date from their current BAU LMS instance (i.e. 01/Jan/2010) or should it be a new date. I ask this question because we will be migrated Learning History records to the new SF-LMS system. Therefore my thinking is that any Curriculas that need to be migrated to the new LMS as well as the learning history records (relating to Learners learning history being migrated from AS-IS LMS) should be based on the same Curriculum date that is maintained in SF-BizX (01/Jan/2010), as opposed to configuring a new date. Please advise

entry.title: Is there an option to view which projects have been modified most recently using a filter
entry.content:
Is there a way to view directly in the project management module which projects have been modified the last time? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is there an option to view which projects have been m...
entry.content: The advanced filter for projects does not include the "Changed On" date. As a result, you are unable to see which projects have been most recently modified.

entry.title: Why is final confirmation, no remaining work, and clear open res. automatically checked off in IW41
entry.content:
When entering in time confirmations in IW41, the final confirmation, no remaining work and clear open res. are automatically checked off.

How do I update the system so when I go into IW41 these boxes are not checked off?

entry.title: Re: Add an accounting document to an SD sales flow
entry.content: Thank you, you helped me with many questions! So if I want to add an accounting document after the billing processing, it should be something Z.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit : Migrate your Data using Staging Tables a CO-Cost Center Object c
entry.content: @Caio Thank you for enriching the article, your contribution is very valuable.

entry.title: Re: Memo Line rules in SAP ByD
entry.content:
Hi ,

Basically there is no option to maintain memo line rules for bank accounts. A particular company has a bank account , but the bank account should not have a company.

Try to check this documentation guide for creation of memo line rule: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/0635ec3491974ad988be05d6b1dcf734/2cb9b071722d1014bf6fcf9dad622515.html?locale=en-US

Best Regards,

SAP Technical Support, Public Cloud ERP

entry.title: Re: Cannot open SAPUI5 Fiori Applications after set openM...
entry.content:
You have to be specific, "doesn't work" means what?

I think you need to use embedIntoDetailsNestedRouter if you want to load your detail page to the inbox

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Polish SAF-T Regulatory Requirements
entry.content:
We are thinking of implementing SAP and are therefore looking for a solution for our Polish subsidiary to cover the requirements of the Polish tax authorities for mandatory SAF-T reporting. As we plan to use SAP S/4HANA public cloud, the SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T Regulatory Requirements cannot be used (as far as I understood).

In 2023 ‘SAP Document and Reporting Compliance’ was not yet able to cover the requirements of the Polish authorities. But what about today?

In this webinar (Standard Audit File-Tax (SAF-T) - Statutory Reporting in Poland (JPK) - SAP Product Strategy) here at the end of the last year there was an item on the agenda called "'Transition from SAP Add-on for Polish SAF-T Regulatory Requirements’ to ‘SAP Document and Reporting Compliance’".

entry.title: Re: SAP Power Designer Excel Bulk Upload on Entity > Mapp...
entry.content:
Hi Vipul,

I am afraid, that you are asking too much (from PD Excel import). I`ve tried several times to import mappings using standard Excel Import extension and my each conclusion was that it cannot be done.

As a result, I created my own automated mappings import from Excel. Pros and cons are obvious. You can tailor the script for importing mappings to your current Excel structure. However, you still have to take into account mandatory items such as Data Sources, Attribute mapping cannot exist without a parent Entity mapping, etc. Disadvantage is also clear. You have to spend time by writing the import script. And believe me, importing mappings is not a matter of 200 lines of VB script. There are surprisingly many things/rules you have check to make sure, your imported data are consistent and PD-valid.

Unlike me, you have the great advantage of being able to use my previously acquired knowledge and save time doing your own import. Let me know on LinkedIn if you are interested.

Have a nice day,

Ondrej Divis

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center: New way to view, download and transport configurations
entry.content: Thank you @hariprasadgud , I raised 77464/2025 for it.

entry.title: Re: Disenchantment: GPT for generating ABAP code documentation
entry.content:
I think you are an expert trying to see if ChatGPT would do a better job than you could, at a highly specialized task. I don't think that's what AI is currently aiming at, at all.

I use AI in my personal life regularly to help me write emails for example to my children's doctors and teachers, especially because I'm not a very good writer in German, and it's amazing. Now, would a professional translator do a better job at this task? Very probably. And maybe there are professional translators doing the same exercise you are doing, sharing with other translators that AI is still doing an inferior job to what they would do, but they are missing the point, aren't they? The point is that, in many situations, people don't need perfect. People don't need the best. Many times, people just need "good enough". And this will definitely lower the workload for many professionals around the world.
